fix: fetch article content from URL + angleInstruction support for compare voices (#105)

* [claude-code] feat: add legacy bcrypt+JWT auth fallback for register and login

P0 fix — users were blocked from logging in when Supabase auth failed.
Now register and login both try Supabase first, then fall back to local
bcrypt password hashing + JWT token generation. Registration always
stores passwordHash for dual-mode support. Refresh endpoint handles
legacy JWT re-signing when no refresh token exists.



* [claude-code] fix: seed-anil stores bcrypt passwordHash for legacy login fallback



* [claude-code] docs: QA pass results — 2026-04-03 TESTING lane

- Backend build: PASS (Prisma + tsc clean)
- Backend tests: 258/260 pass (2 auth.test.ts failures, not regression)
- Frontend build: FAIL (stale .next cache, ENOENT pages-manifest.json)
- Backend health: PASS (Railway prod ok)
- Frontend health: PASS (Vercel prod 200)



* [claude-code] fix: add Zod validation to unvalidated POST/PATCH handlers

- drafts: createDraftSchema + updateDraftSchema replace raw req.body destructuring
- analytics: learningLogSchema replaces manual event check



* [claude-code] fix: replace console.warn/error with pino logger in auth, redis, supabase, pipeline, providers

Scripts (seed, smoke-test) intentionally keep console.log for CLI output.



* [claude-code] fix: mount loopRouter at /api/loop — was defined but never registered

Fixes loop.state and loop.createPR returning 404. Found by Cluster 4 endpoint audit.



* [claude-code] docs: QA results — 2026-04-02 Run 2

Backend build PASS, tests 257/260 (flaky timeouts), production healthy.



* [claude-code] feat: add POST /drafts/:id/refine and POST /voice/calibrate routes

- drafts.refine: takes instruction, runs generation pipeline with it as
  feedback, updates draft in-place with refined content
- voice.calibrate: fetches tweets by handle via Twitter API, runs Claude
  calibration, upserts voice profile with calibrated dimensions

Both routes were wired in the frontend (api.ts) but had no backend
endpoints. Loop router was already mounted in a prior commit.



* fix: update auth tests for bcrypt fallback — register returns 200, login returns 401

The legacy bcrypt auth fallback (commit 046d2c8) changed behavior when
Supabase is null: register now succeeds via bcrypt, login returns 401
when user not found. Tests expected 503 from the old Supabase-only path.

- Alert ownership: GET/PATCH/DELETE /:id now verify userId ownership (CODEX-01)
- Auth tests: updated for dual-mode Supabase + legacy JWT fallback, mock bcrypt
- Zod validation: added input schemas to drafts, images, research, voice routes
- New tests: alerts ownership, rateLimit middleware, voice, research, images routes

* feat: expand voice calibration to all 12 dimensions

Previously calibrateFromTweets only scored 4 dimensions (humor,
formality, brevity, contrarianTone). Now scores all 12 including

solutionOrientation, socialPosture, selfPromotionalIntensity.

Calibrate endpoint persists all 12 to VoiceProfile. Maturity now
auto-detects ADVANCED at 100+ tweets analyzed.

- POST /api/drafts/:id/refine — takes custom instruction, generates
  refined version using AI pipeline, saves as new draft version

- Unlocks full demo flow: Generate → Refine → Post to X → Analytics



* feat: add content scheduling — schedule drafts for auto-posting

- Add SCHEDULED status to DraftStatus enum
- Add scheduledAt field to TweetDraft model
- POST /api/drafts/:id/schedule — set future post time
- POST /api/drafts/process-scheduled — cron endpoint to auto-post due drafts
- Index on (status, scheduledAt) for efficient scheduled draft queries

* fix: thread test assertions — use data.count/data.thread for success wrapper



* fix: wrap alerts, drafts, x-auth responses in standard success() envelope

Frontend API client expects {ok, data, timestamp} envelope on all endpoints.
alerts.ts, drafts.ts, and x-auth.ts were returning bare data objects.
Updated tests to use res.body.data.* assertions.



* test: add briefing preferences endpoint tests (#47)

Covers GET/PUT /api/briefing/preferences with 8 test cases:
auth, null preference, existing preference, upsert, validation, errors.




* test: add voice calibration endpoint tests (#48)

Covers POST /api/voice/calibrate with 11 test cases:
auth, validation, Twitter fetch mock, Claude calibration,
maturity levels (BEGINNER/INTERMEDIATE/ADVANCED), error handling.




* test: add X OAuth endpoint tests (#49)

Covers POST /authorize, POST /callback, GET /status, POST /disconnect
with 10 test cases: auth, PKCE flow, token exchange, status checks.




* feat: normalize all 12 voice dimensions to 0-100 scale (#46)

Previously, the 4 core dimensions (humor, formality, brevity, contrarianTone)
used 0-100 while the 8 extended dimensions used 0-10. This mismatch caused
the frontend (which uses 0-100 for all) to send values that failed backend
validation.

Changes:
- Prisma schema: Float→Int, default 5.0→50 for 8 extended dimensions
- Zod validation: max(10)→max(100) for 8 extended dimensions
- Prompt builder: toTenPointScale() converts 0-100 stored values to 0-10
  for natural language descriptions (describe functions unchanged)
- Calibration: AI still returns 0-10, tenToHundred() converts for storage




* feat: background draft scheduler — auto-posts scheduled drafts every 60s

Polls for SCHEDULED drafts with scheduledAt <= now, posts to X via OAuth,
handles token refresh, moves to APPROVED if no X token linked. Processes
max 10 per cycle. Runs inside main API process (no separate worker).



* fix: use grok-3-latest model for trending scan

* fix: onboardingTrack enum binary format error in registration

Prisma was encoding `undefined` as the OnboardingTrack enum type,
causing PostgreSQL error "incorrect binary data format in bind parameter 7".
Changed to conditional spread so the field is omitted entirely when not provided.

* feat: Oracle chat endpoint (Phase 3)

POST /api/oracle/message — LLM-powered Oracle responses for
personalized onboarding commentary.

New files:
- routes/oracle.ts — endpoint with Zod validation, 8s timeout,
  graceful fallback if LLM fails
- lib/oracle-prompt.ts — Oracle personality system prompt +
  calibration commentary, blend preview, dimension reactions

Provider routing:
- oracle_smart (Anthropic → OpenAI → Gemini) for creative/commentary
- oracle_fast (same chain, lower tokens) for quick acks

LLM is only called for specific steps (calibration results,
blend preview, unusual dimension combos, free text). All other
messages remain scripted client-side.

### Motivation
- Reduce Dependabot noise by rolling up multiple open, passing
Dependabot Go module updates into a single, non-breaking dependency bump
PR.
- Keep the module graph consistent and resolve transitive changes so
local development and CI see the same versions.
- Validate changes using the repository Makefile-native workflow and
sign off with the project DCO policy.

### Description
- Applied the Dependabot-targeted minor/patch bumps and compatible
transitive updates in `go.mod`/`go.sum`, including (not exhaustive)
`github.com/cli/cli/v2` → `v2.89.0`, `github.com/mark3labs/mcp-go` →
`v0.47.0`, `github.com/yuin/goldmark` → `v1.8.2`, `golang.org/x/oauth2`
→ `v0.36.0`, `google.golang.org/api` → `v0.274.0`, `golang.org/x/net` →
`v0.52.0`, `github.com/fatih/color` → `v1.19.0`,
`github.com/go-playground/validator/v10` → `v10.30.2`, and
`go.opentelemetry.io/contrib/instrumentation/net/http/otelhttp` →
`v0.67.0`.
- Updated `go.sum` via dependency resolution and ran `go mod tidy` to
clean the module graph and checksum file.
- Performed dependency upgrades with `go get -t -u ./...` and recorded
the resulting module set changes in `go.mod`/`go.sum`.
- Changes were committed with DCO signoff and are available in the
working branch at commit `ae9bcfefedf486335008047cf8ea787beaea8f1b`.

### Testing
- Ran `go get -t -u ./...` which completed and upgraded direct and
transitive modules successfully.
- Ran `go mod tidy` which completed and normalized `go.mod`/`go.sum`
successfully.
- Ran `make install/dev` which completed (tool install steps ran, though
the environment lacks a git tag description and some tool binaries may
be placed under the container GOPATH).
- Ran `make lint` which failed due to a missing vet tool binary: `go vet
-vettool=/root/go/bin/checklocks ...` could not find

environment/tooling/runtime expectations (examples include missing
`deno`, interpreters/tooling for some file-command tests producing empty
output, and tests asserting specific version/frontmatter metadata that
differ in this ephemeral environment).

ref: Improve architecture with boundary enforcement and service extraction (#159)

Improve the junior package architecture by splitting oversized
orchestration modules into focused services, extracting isolated build
and tool-execution helpers, and replacing bespoke boundary enforcement
with maintained tooling.

The original motivation was to reduce the fix-chain pattern around
`respond.ts`, `agent-tools.ts`, and `nitro.ts`, where unrelated
responsibilities were coupled closely enough that small changes tended
to cascade. This branch moves post-execution result handling, checkpoint
persistence, and MCP auth pause handling into dedicated chat services;
breaks Nitro build concerns into small `build/` modules; and extracts
tool execution helpers so sandbox input construction, credential
injection, result normalization, and tool error handling are
independently testable.

The branch also tightens the supporting architecture around that
refactor. Chat tool availability now flows through explicit channel
capabilities instead of implicit conversation-scoped checks. GenAI
logging and tracing semantics are aligned with the updated logging
specs, and the empty-response diagnostic path keeps generated file
counts intact. The PR also adds the missing JSDoc on
`generateAssistantReply`, restores lost context comments, and keeps the
architecture and plugin specs in sync with the code.

Since the branch now owns architecture enforcement, it no longer uses
the custom `check-chat-architecture-boundary.mjs` script. Instead,
`@sentry/junior` uses `oxlint` for package linting and
`dependency-cruiser` for chat import boundary rules, with `lint-staged`
running `oxlint --fix` before Prettier on staged JS and TS files. This
is simpler to maintain and catches the relative-import boundary cases
the custom script missed.

A few pre-existing failures and test-shape issues were fixed while
landing the refactor. The MCP progressive loading test now lives at the
integration layer, example build discovery refreshes the pnpm install
after rebuilding the package, the webhook lazy-load test has a less
brittle timeout, and several focused unit tests were rewritten so they
fail deterministically instead of relying on conditional assertions or
unsafe optional chaining.

The result is still one branch, but the current shape is narrower and
more explicit than where it started: smaller module surfaces,
architecture checks backed by standard tooling, and a clean `pnpm
--filter @sentry/junior lint` baseline for follow-on work.

commit.message:
Merge PR #2: Julia MCWF migration

### Motivation

- Port the MATLAB Monte Carlo wavefunction (MCWF / MC quantum jumps) implementation to Julia using `QuantumOptics.jl` and provide a high-performance, thread-parallel core optimized for production runs and comparisons with the MATLAB reference.
- Provide reproducible project configuration and developer tooling (instantiation, benchmark, MATLAB-compatibility verification, and a Jupyter entry notebook) to ease validation and server-scale parameter scans.

### Description

- Add a new Julia package structure with `Project.toml` and `Manifest.toml` and a top-level module `src/QuantumMC.jl` that exposes the `MCWFOptimized` submodule.
- Implement the MCWF core in `src/MCWFOptimized.jl` including `MCWFParams`/`RunConfig` types, per-thread `TrajCache`, `run_trajectory!` single-trajectory logic, and `run_ensemble` threaded execution with thread-local RNGs and caches.
- Fix numerical/physics issues versus the original port: use the full kinetic energy expression including the cross term, handle possibly-negative discriminants by converting to `ComplexF64` in `_ep32`, and perform in-place, allocation-free operations (`mul!`, `copyto!`, preallocated buffers) to reduce GC pressure.
- Add documentation, usage and operational guides in `README.md` and `docs/` (Chinese), a Jupyter notebook `notebooks/QuantumMC_入口.ipynb`, and utility scripts `scripts/verify_against_matlab.jl` and `scripts/benchmark.jl` for correctness verification against a MATLAB `.mat` reference and for benchmarking (including optional MATLAB `parfor` speedup comparison).

### Testing

- This PR does not include CI test runs and no automated tests were executed as part of the push.
- Added verification and benchmark helpers: run `scripts/verify_against_matlab.jl` (requires a `matlab_reference.mat` with `probability_ref`) for correctness and `scripts/benchmark.jl` for performance measurement; these scripts are intended for local/CI execution.
- Package instantiation is supported via `julia --project -e 'using Pkg; Pkg.instantiate()'` to prepare dependencies before running the verification or benchmark scripts.
- No test failures were reported because no automated tests were run in this PR.

commit.message:
Work (#9)

* Add contacts import (Google Sheets/YClients), DB migrations, and clients/staff/settings UI/hooks updates

### Motivation
- Provide a robust path to import contacts from either YClients or Google Sheets and persist import metadata in org settings.
- Make clients UI more feature-rich (age/discount filters, wider table with left-accent scrollbar) and keep staff UI in sync with real masters data from the DB.
- Ensure database schema supports YClients-compatible client fields and automatic linking between user records and clients when YClients IDs appear.

### Description
- Added an API endpoint `app/api/clients/import/route.ts` that normalizes rows from `yclients` or `google_sheets`, upserts/inserts into `clients`, and writes import metadata into `org_settings`.
- Implemented `lib/imports/normalizeGoogleSheetsClient.ts` to adapt Google Sheets rows into the clients schema and added `normalizeYclientsRow` logic in the import route.
- Introduced DB migrations and helper SQL: `add_contacts_import_source.sql`, `add_yclients_clients_columns.sql`, `link_users_to_clients_on_yc_id.sql`, seed and reset updates and a seeds file `seed_test_masters.sql` to populate masters for staff cards.
- Updated frontend: extended clients filters and table (`app/clients/page.tsx`) with age/discount filters, redesigned table layout and local left-accent scrollbar (`app/globals.css`), added settings UI to pick and save import source (`app/settings/page.tsx`), and disabled/marked in-development auto-systems in `app/system/page.tsx`.
- Refactored data hooks: `lib/hooks/useClients.ts` now reads from `clients` and normalizes new fields (discount, channels, birthday, etc.), and added a new `lib/hooks/useYclientsMasters.ts` hook to fetch/subscribe to `masters` for the staff UI; `app/staff/page.tsx` updated to consume masters and render realistic KPIs and cards.

### Testing
- Ran TypeScript/Next.js build (`next build`) and static type-checking locally and the build completed without type errors.
- Ran linter/format checks (ESLint) and no lint errors were reported for modified files.
- Performed basic automated smoke checks by running the dev server and loading `Clients`, `Staff`, and `Settings` pages to ensure hooks and new components render without runtime errors (no automated unit tests were changed or present for these new features).

* Store messenger user IDs on clients during yc_id auto-link

* Add reverse sync from clients to messenger user tables
